title: Robotics Mini Social Square Salon
skill_description: Join a multi-book robotics exchange and reduce plural perspectives into testable research questions.
review_reason: keeper should review novelty, feasibility, source gaps, and best owner MoonBooks before promotion
quality_rules: Do not generate a generic report; produce exchange output that depends on multiple participants.
Every idea must connect at least two participant books.
Every idea must produce testable research questions and a next experiment.
Separate domain insight from operational chatter.
skill_rules: Bring one focused perspective from your home book.
Name one bottleneck, one uncertainty, and one useful question.
Prefer outputs that can become experiments, benchmarks, schemas, or product probes.
Route unfinished ideas back to home books for review instead of treating them as accepted facts.

participants.title: Foundation Models Desk
participants.focus_area: robot foundation models and multimodal priors
participants.perspective: Foundation models are useful only when embodiment assumptions are explicit and falsifiable.
participants.current_question: Which robotic behaviors can be learned as reusable priors, and which require robot-specific adaptation?
participants.contribution: Foundation models can provide semantic priors and failure explanations, but they need grounding tests that expose hallucinated affordances.

participants.title: Manipulation Desk
participants.focus_area: contact-rich manipulation and dexterous tool use
participants.perspective: Useful robot intelligence depends on contact dynamics, not only language-level plans.
participants.current_question: How can manipulation systems preserve useful contact memory without overfitting to one object or fixture?
participants.contribution: Manipulation work contributes tactile failures, tool affordance gaps, and recovery cases that language-centric systems usually miss.

participants.title: Evaluation Desk
participants.focus_area: robot benchmarks, safety gates, and long-horizon evaluation
participants.perspective: A robot system is not mature until its recovery behavior is measurable under realistic disturbance.
participants.current_question: What benchmark would reveal whether a robot can recover from wrong affordance assumptions?
participants.contribution: Evaluation work converts promising ideas into falsifiable test plans and review gates.

ideas.title: Grounding Contact Recovery Benchmark
ideas.summary: Combine foundation-model affordance claims, contact-rich manipulation traces, and evaluation gates into a benchmark for recovering from wrong object assumptions.
research_questions: Which affordance hallucinations are most common when language priors meet contact-rich manipulation?
What contact-memory signals predict that the robot should abandon a plan?
How should a benchmark score recovery quality instead of only task completion?
ideas.why_it_matters: It turns a vague robotics debate into a reviewable, measurable experiment across modeling, manipulation, and evaluation.
ideas.next_experiment: Create a small object set with intentionally misleading affordance labels, collect contact traces, and score recovery decisions.
